summary: South Korea's transition is led by the Ministry of Science and ICT, which is building a national post-quantum cryptography transition infrastructure including a national PQC body, with budget allocated for the move. The Korea Internet and Security Agency runs the annual sector-by-sector PQC pilot transition programme, which began in 2025 with energy, medical and public administration and was extended in 2026 to telecom, finance, transport, defence and space, and it has now started constructing a joint-certificate public-key infrastructure to support the transition. Korean guidance applies both the NIST post-quantum standards and domestic Korean post-quantum algorithms.
migrationTimeline:
2025 | Sector-by-sector PQC pilot transition support programme launched (energy, medical, public administration)
2026 | PQC pilot transition project extended to telecom, finance, transport, defence and space sectors, with a re-recruitment of participating companies
2026 | Construction of a joint-certificate PKI infrastructure for the PQC transition, running through 30 November 2026
govActors:
MSIT | Ministry of Science and ICT, establishing the national post-quantum cryptography transition infrastructure and a national PQC body, with budget allocated for the transition, and co-issuing the 2026 pilot transition re-recruitment
KISA | Korea Internet & Security Agency, reference guidance on post-quantum cryptography, the annual sector-by-sector PQC pilot transition support programmes, and the construction of joint-certificate PKI infrastructure for the transition
mainRegulation:
KCMVP cryptographic module validation (government) | national | binding-by-market-access
KpqC national algorithm selections (2025: NTRU+, SMAUG-T, AIMer, HAETAE) | national | guidance
MSIT national PQC transition programme (2025-2035) | national | guidance
obligation: Government cryptographic modules must pass KCMVP validation, which selects the national KpqC algorithms; the MSIT programme runs phased sector pilots to 2035. There is no economy-wide statutory mandate.
classificationBasis:
summary + govActors (MSIT): MSIT national PQC transition infrastructure row and the 2026 KRW 8.12 trillion budget row
govActors (KISA): KISA PQC reference page and the 2025 and 2026 pilot transition call rows
migrationTimeline 2025/2026: the 2025 (energy, medical, public administration) and 2026 (telecom, finance, transport, defence, space) pilot transition rows. These are programme phases rather than a national completion timeline
dominantProcess Mixed: a KISA included candidate description applies post-quantum cryptography labelled NIST PQC/KPQC, and a separate KISA briefing references the NIST PQC effort, while MSIT pursues national/sovereign PQC infrastructure. Korea also runs its own KpqC national algorithm competition, but the dedicated KpqC Research Group documents are included:false and not relied on. Mixed assigned because both the NIST camp and a domestic Korean scheme are attested in included material; a Sovereign reading is plausible if the KpqC scheme is confirmed - REVIEW
targetCompletion: OMITTED, no national completion date stated in the included summaries (a national master plan is referenced only in an included:false KpqC notice)
standardFamilies / algorithms: OMITTED, no specific FIPS or algorithm names stated in the included results summaries
